Settings.Update.Detail.InvalidManifest: Update metadata is unavailable. You can use the mirror download.
Settings.Update.Detail.HashMissing: The update package has no verification data. Use the mirror download.
Settings.Update.Detail.HashMismatch: The update package failed verification. Download again or use the mirror download.
Settings.Update.Detail.DownloadMissing: The installer is missing. Download the update again.
Settings.Update.Detail.StoreReady: This build is updated by Microsoft Store. You can also check manually here.
Settings.Update.Detail.StoreNotPackaged: This is not a Store package, so Microsoft Store updates are unavailable.
Settings.Update.Detail.StoreCanceled: The Microsoft Store update was canceled.
Settings.Update.Detail.StoreInstallFaile: Microsoft Store did not complete the update. Try again later or check updates in Store.
Settings.Update.Detail.StoreUnavailable: Microsoft Store is unavailable right now. Try again later or open Store to check updates.
Settings.Update.InstallConfirmBody: DeskBox will save settings, exit, install the new version with the update helper, and restart.
Settings.Update.InstallStartFailedBody: Download the update again, or install it manually from GitHub Releases.

{0}
Settings.Dialog.RestoreBody: Appearance, display, interaction, hotkey, and feature widget preferences will be restored. Language, startup, feature widget switches, existing widgets and positions, storage paths, Quick Capture content, Todo tasks, and files will be preserved.
Settings.Dialog.ProductReasonP1: Windows desktop has always been one of the most-used workspaces. It is close at hand, but it also tends to collect temporary files, shortcuts, screenshots, and things waiting to be handled. Over time, it becomes a place with everything on it, yet nothing is easy to find.
Settings.Dialog.ProductReasonP2: DeskBox started as a lightweight and restrained way to make the desktop cleaner, more organized, and easier to use again. It does not try to replace the Windows desktop. It sits beside existing habits and helps collect frequent items, temporary content, and small tools more naturally.
Settings.Dialog.ProductReasonP3: I genuinely like the native feel of WinUI, and I care about whether a tool feels like it belongs on Windows. DeskBox will keep following native design, interaction, and visual patterns as it evolves.
Settings.Dialog.ProductReasonP4: When a system-native capability can solve a problem, DeskBox will prefer it. When a third-party dependency is not necessary, DeskBox will avoid adding it. This may take more care, but it helps keep the app stable, light, and close to the system.
Settings.Dialog.ProductReasonP5: DeskBox is not trying to pile on features. It focuses on stability, performance, simplicity, and comfort in long-term use. The installer is relatively large mainly because of WinUI 3, Windows App SDK, and .NET baseline size, not because it includes many unrelated features.
Onboarding.Intro.Title: DeskBox
Onboarding.Intro.Body: Add a lightweight organizing layer to the Windows desktop for viewing, storing, and quickly accessing everyday content.
Onboarding.Step1.Title: Add a lightweight layer to your desktop
Onboarding.Step1.Body: DeskBox does not replace your Windows desktop. It puts files, todos, and everyday tools into lightweight widgets you can summon when needed and dismiss when not.
Onboarding.Step1.Hint1: Keep the normal Windows desktop and file behavior
Onboarding.Step1.Hint2: Widgets can be freely moved and resized
Onboarding.Step2.Title: Where do dragged files go?
Onboarding.Step2.Body: Files dropped into a managed widget move to the DeskBox folder. Pin it to Explorer's sidebar so you can quickly find it in any upload dialog.
Onboarding.Step2.Hint1: Mapped widgets also create shortcuts in the DeskBox folder
Onboarding.Step2.Hint2: Path and pin status can be changed anytime in Settings
Onboarding.Step2.PinDescription: Once pinned, you can quickly reach the DeskBox folder from the left side of any file upload dialog
Onboarding.Step3.Title: Choose your look
Onboarding.Step3.Body: Widgets stay on your desktop, so pick a style you like. You can always change it later in Settings.
Onboarding.Step3.Hint1: The accent color affects highlight elements in widgets
Onboarding.Step4.Title: Put everyday tools on the desktop
Onboarding.Step4.Body: Besides the file widget, you can enable these lightweight feature widgets. They will be created on your desktop automatically.
Onboarding.Step4.Hint1: A file widget is always created — no need to select
Onboarding.Step4.Hint2: Feature widgets can be toggled anytime in Settings
Onboarding.Step4.TodoDescription: Light task management with due dates and reminders
Onboarding.Step4.QuickCaptureDescription: Quickly record text and links, with optional clipboard capture
Onboarding.Step4.MusicDescription: Control media playback with artwork-derived colors
Onboarding.Step4.WeatherDescription: Real-time weather and 7-day forecast with auto location
Onboarding.Step5.Title: Summon and dismiss anytime
Onboarding.Step5.Body: Widgets won't stay in your way. Use the tray icon or a hotkey to show or hide all widgets at any time.
Onboarding.Step5.Hint1: Left-click the tray icon to show or hide
Onboarding.Step5.Hint2: You can change the shortcut later in Settings
Onboarding.Step5.HotkeyDescription: Press the hotkey to quickly show or hide all widgets
Onboarding.Step5.StartupDescription: Start DeskBox after signing in to Windows
Onboarding.Step6.Title: Your desktop workspace is ready
Onboarding.Step6.Body: An empty file widget has been created on your desktop. Try dragging a file into it.
Onboarding.Step6.Hint1: Drag desktop files into the widget to organize them
Onboarding.Step6.Hint2: When uploading files, find the DeskBox folder in Explorer's Quick Access
Onboarding.Step6.Hint3: Right-click the tray icon for more actions
